about.description1: Altruvex is a precision web engineering firm in Cairo. We build multilingual web systems for businesses that need architecture - not templates with a brand coat.
about.description2: Every project receives direct founder involvement. No outsourcing. No theme builders. No intermediary between the decision and the engineer.

founder.name: Ali Abdelhadi
founder.philosophy1: Altruvex is being built to become the reference standard in its category - a company recognized for engineering precision, structural clarity, and execution that holds under scrutiny.
founder.philosophy2: The goal is not to appear premium. It is to build work with sufficient quality and positioning that others can copy the surface - but not the standard behind it.

stat2.label: Recycled Code

demo.ar_body: هذا نص توضيحي يظهر كيف يتكيف التصميم تماماً مع اتجاه الكتابة من اليمين إلى اليسار. كل عنصر يُعاد تصميمه، وليس مجرد انعكاس.
demo.en_body: This is example text showing how the design adapts completely to right-to-left direction. Every element is reconsidered, not just mirrored.
demo.title: Interactive Demo

multilingual.paragraphs: Most sites treat Multilingual RTL as an afterthought - flip the layout and hope it holds. We design systems where direction is a first-class architectural decision from day one. This is not about translation. It is cultural adaptation. Right-aligned navigation is not mirrored - it is reconsidered. Every component, every layout, every interaction - built to perform in both directions without compromise.
multilingual.title: Structure that respects direction.

items.1: Accept projects with fixed deadlines imposed before the scope is understood
items.2: Build systems we cannot stand behind technically
items.3: Work through agencies that sit between us and the end client
items.5: Start execution before the problem is fully mapped

closing.description: Not every project needs this level of thinking. Some need speed. Some need iteration. But if you are building something that needs to last - if the system is the product, not just the storefront - we should talk. No pitch. No pressure. A direct technical discussion about what you are building and whether we are the right fit.
closing.title: If this matches how you think,

constraints.paragraphs: Unlimited options create paralysis, not products. We begin every project by defining what we will not build - which features are out of scope, which use cases are excluded, which decisions are off the table. This is not limitation. It is focus. A system designed for everyone serves no one well. Constraints force decisions. Decisions create character. Character builds a product with a point of view.
constraints.title: Constraints are a design tool.

1.altruvex: Understand the problem users are actually trying to solve
1.common: Build the features users asked for

2.altruvex: Define architectural constraints before opening the editor

3.altruvex: Launch is when the system starts working - not when fixes start
3.common: We will fix it after launch

data.description: Before UI, before features, before brand - we map what the system needs to know. A weak data model cannot be rescued by clean code on top of it.
data.title: Data First

handoff.description: Code written for one person is fragile. Every commit assumes someone else will read and extend it six months from now.
handoff.title: Write for the Next Engineer

maintenance.description: A system that requires constant attention is not finished. We optimize for long stretches of stability where nothing breaks, nothing drifts.
maintenance.title: Minimize Intervention Cost

scale.description: Not because every project will reach it - but because growth assumptions change every architectural decision made today.
scale.title: Design for 10x

hero.description: Interface, architecture, copy, and CTA structure all emerge from one question: what must the buyer understand, and what should they do next?
hero.title: We do not design pages first.

card2.body: Fixed-scope Technical Audit at 15,000 EGP. Project investment ranges published. No scope creep disguised as discovery.

card3.body: RTL is not a CSS override here. Our architecture treats every supported language as a first-class citizen - structurally equal from day one.

authority.subtitle: Until client testimonials are earned, this is the proof you can inspect directly on the page in front of you.

altruvex-site.name: Altruvex.com - The Site Itself Had to Be the Proof
altruvex-site.outcome: Sub-1s mobile TTI, RTL switching in under 16ms with zero layout shift, and leads that arrive pre-qualified - with scope and investment already mapped before the first call begins.
altruvex-site.problem: In a market where agencies compete on price, Altruvex needed its own site to prove technical quality without borrowing enterprise credibility it had not yet earned.
altruvex-site.solution: We built a multilingual site on a custom Next.js stack with native RTL architecture, performance-first frontend engineering, and a transparency flow that qualifies leads before the first conversation.
altruvex-site.summary: A multilingual proof build designed to demonstrate technical quality, native RTL design, and lead qualification before the first call.

art-lighting-store.name: Art Lighting Store
art-lighting-store.outcome: A production storefront with high-resolution product imagery, synchronized inventory, and a handoff the client team can operate without engineering support.
art-lighting-store.problem: The client sold high-end fixtures where material and finish detail matter, but their existing platform compressed images aggressively and loaded slowly. Customers could not properly inspect finishes or scale, and inventory was fragmented across multiple systems - creating overselling risk.
art-lighting-store.solution: We designed a custom Next.js storefront with an image pipeline tuned for high-resolution zoom without sacrificing load time. Inventory is synchronized via webhooks into a single source of truth, and pages are statically generated with targeted revalidation for fast, consistent delivery.
art-lighting-store.summary: High-resolution product imagery at scale with real-time inventory and fast page loads for a premium lighting retailer.

newlight-lighting-store.name: NewLight Lighting Store - Venturing into E-Commerce
newlight-lighting-store.outcome: NewLight's first online store launched on custom architecture with multilingual journeys, payments, and a catalog the team can manage day to day.
newlight-lighting-store.problem: As a leading lighting company, NewLight needed to transition their traditional showroom into a digital experience that allows customers to browse and buy easily while maintaining the brand's premium feel.
newlight-lighting-store.solution: We designed a custom e-commerce store focused on visual aesthetics. We built a flexible product management system and a structured checkout flow, with full multilingual support across all devices.
newlight-lighting-store.summary: When NewLight decided to launch their first online store, we built a custom e-commerce platform that matches their brand and supports multilingual buying on every device.

pricingSignal.title: Transparent pricing. No hidden costs.

exclusions.label: Quoted Separately
exclusions.value: Branding from scratch, copywriting, paid stock, SMS, email credits, premium SaaS tools, and third-party subscriptions are not included unless listed in scope.

paymentTerms.label: Payment Terms
paymentTerms.value: 50% to start · 30% at milestone · 20% before launch No deposit = no project start.

yearOne.label: Year 1 Setup
yearOne.value: Domain, SSL, deployment, and base hosting for Year 1 are included for standard website scopes. Higher-load infrastructure is quoted only when the project requires it.

pricing.description: The tiers below help buyers self-qualify quickly before opening full Transparency.
pricing.ownershipNote: Every project includes full source code ownership - not a Shopify subscription, not a WordPress plugin stack. You own everything, permanently.

01.a: Architecture, workflows, integrations, and multilingual requirements drive the number - not arbitrary metrics like page count. We price against current custom-development market rates and the engineering complexity of the system.
01.q: How do you determine the investment range?

02.a: No hidden fees. Year 1 includes domain, SSL, deployment, and base hosting where relevant. Renewals, paid third-party tools, and channels like SMS are only billed if your scope actually uses them.

03.a: Milestone-based: a commencement deposit to secure the build window, then payments tied to discovery completion, design approval, and development delivery.
03.q: What does the payment schedule look like?

04.a: Yes. Every project is built on modular architecture so it can grow. Start with a Focused Website and layer on authentication, payments, or dashboards as the business evolves.
04.q: Can I upgrade my system tier later?

05.a: Yes. Our Focused Website tier is for companies that need custom work without jumping straight into product-level investment. For more complex systems, we phase the build so the investment matches the business stage.
05.q: Do you work with early-stage companies?

faq.subtitle: Common questions about our investment model and how we scope projects.
faq.title: Pricing Questions

pricing.label: 12 - Investment Logic

roi.calculation: A site rebuilt 18 months after launch because it could not handle integrations, user accounts, or traffic typically costs 2–3× the original build - plus the revenue lost during the gap.
roi.question: The real question is not what this costs to build. It is what the wrong build costs over two years.

timeSaved.label: Your Time Saved
timeSaved.sub: No hiring, managing, or code reviews

1.title: 1. Information We Collect
1.description: When you contact us, request an estimate, or schedule a meeting, we collect minimal information:
1.projectValue: Service interest, project timeline, project type, complexity level
1.techValue: IP address (for security and rate limiting), browser type, device information

2.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
2.description: We use the information we collect for these specific purposes only:
2.item1: Respond to your inquiries and provide project investment estimates
2.item3: Improve our website performance and user experience
2.item4: Protect against spam and abuse (rate limiting, honeypot validation)

3.title: 3. Data Storage & Security
3.description:
Your data is stored securely and we're explicit about how:

• **Database**: PostgreSQL with SSL encryption for all data in transit and at rest
• **Hosting**: Vercel for application layer, with DDoS protection and automatic backups
• **Backups**: Daily automated backups, retained for 30 days
• **Access Control**: Encrypted environment variables; only essential team members have database access
• **Data Retention**: Contact information kept for 2 years after last contact; you can request deletion anytime

We do not sell, trade, or transfer your personal information to third parties. We also do not use your data to train AI models or for marketing beyond direct project communication.

4.title: 4. Your Rights
4.description: You have explicit control over your data:
4.item1: Request access to your personal data (we'll provide a data export within 5 business days)
4.item2: Request correction or deletion of your data (with exceptions for legal/compliance records)
4.item3: Withdraw consent at any time (you can opt out of all communications)
4.item4: Lodge a complaint with the Egyptian Data Protection Authority or relevant data protection authority in your jurisdiction

5.title: 5. Cookies & Tracking
5.description:
We use minimal tracking:

• **Essential Cookies**: Site functionality only (language preference, session ID)
• **No Third-Party Tracking**: We don't use Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, or similar platforms
• **No User Profiling**: We don't build behavioral profiles or sell data to advertisers

If we ever implement analytics in the future, we will use privacy-first tools (like Fathom or Plausible) and provide explicit opt-in consent.

6.title: 6. Project Data & Client Ownership
6.description:
When you hire Altruvex to build your system:

• **Code Ownership**: You own 100% of the source code. We deliver it in a Git repository under your control.
• **Database Ownership**: Your databases and all data within them are yours. We provide export formats and migration documentation.
• **Compliance**: We handle GDPR/CCPA on your behalf during the build, but the compliance responsibility transfers to you after handoff.
• **Third-Party Services**: Any services we integrate (Fawry, Vodafone Cash, Stripe, email providers) have their own privacy policies. We choose services that meet Egyptian/EU standards.
• **No Lock-In**: If you switch providers mid-project or after launch, we provide complete documentation so another developer can take over immediately.

7.title: 7. Payment & Security
7.description:
For project payments:

• We use Stripe or Egyptian bank transfers (never storing your card details directly)
• Payment information is processed securely through PCI-DSS compliant systems
• We send payment receipts to your email; we don't retain payment card details
• For local Egyptian payments, we work with authorized payment processors that comply with CBE regulations

8.title: 8. Data Processing & Subprocessors
8.description:
We use these specific tools (not vague 'partners'):

**Infrastructure**: Vercel (US), PostgreSQL hosting (EU or Egypt-region if requested)
**Email**: SendGrid for transactional emails only
**Payment Processing**: Stripe (US), Fawry (Egypt), Vodafone Cash (Egypt)
**Code Hosting**: GitHub (Microsoft)
**Monitoring**: Sentry for error tracking (EU servers, no personal data)
**Communication**: Slack (internal only; we don't share your data)

Each of these services has signed Data Processing Agreements where applicable. You can request our DPA template anytime.

9.title: 9. Contact Us
9.description: For privacy questions, data access requests, or deletion requests, contact us at:

problem.title: Most businesses aren't getting engineering.
problem.subtitle: The Egyptian and Arab web market is saturated with agencies that charge custom rates for commodity output. The problems below are not edge cases - they are the standard.

items.title: Templates sold as custom work.
items.body: Most agencies deliver a $60 WordPress theme with your logo swapped in - billed as bespoke development. The result: a generic shell that communicates nothing about your business and nothing about your standards.

items.title: Performance never measured.
items.body: Sites that look polished in the demo load in 7+ seconds in production. Bloated scripts, unoptimized images, no Core Web Vitals strategy. Google penalizes it. Visitors leave. Revenue leaks silently.

items.title: Zero ownership after delivery.
items.body: Go-live is the exit event. No documentation. No source code handoff. No architecture explanation. When something breaks - and it will - you're back to square one with a new vendor who inherits a codebase nobody can explain.
items.signal: You own the domain. They own the knowledge.

items.title: Built for demos, not for scale.
items.body: Freelancers and small agencies optimize for the approval moment, not the growth phase. Every feature added post-launch becomes a patch on a patch. The architecture was never designed to evolve - because that was never part of the contract.
items.signal: Every new feature costs more than the last

items.title: No technical accountability.
items.body: The person managing your project has never written production code. They relay messages between you and an offshore team, add two weeks to every timeline, and cannot answer a single technical question about how your system actually works.
items.signal: You're paying for a middleman, not an engineer

problem.closingPre: Altruvex is built to be the answer to

form.riskReversal: Free 30-min call · No commitment · Direct founder access

seo.body: A discovery call is not a sales script. It is a focused technical conversation for founders, operators, and teams that need to understand whether a custom website, portal, ecommerce platform, or internal web system is the right next move.

items.a: No. The first call is free and exists to understand the project, risks, and decision you need to make. If the scope is not a fit, we will say that clearly.
items.q: Is the first technical call paid?

items.a: Bring the business goal, the current website or system if one exists, the main user journeys, and any deadlines or budget constraints. A rough brief is enough.
items.q: What should I prepare before the call?

items.a: Most discovery calls take around 30 minutes. Complex rebuilds or systems with integrations may need a follow-up architecture session before a final proposal.
items.q: How long does the call take?

items.a: After the call, we outline the recommended next step: a technical audit, a scoped proposal, a transparency estimate, or a decision not to build yet.

sections.body: We use the call to separate assumptions from real requirements. That means understanding the users, workflows, integrations, content, performance expectations, and operational constraints before recommending a build path.
sections.title: What we clarify on the call

sections.body: This call is useful when the project is serious enough that a generic quote would be misleading. It is especially valuable for bilingual websites, custom Next.js builds, ecommerce systems, dashboards, portals, and rebuild decisions.
points: Businesses with Arabic and English requirements
Operators who need ownership of the code
sections.title: Who should schedule

sections.body: The outcome should be practical. You should leave with a clearer technical direction, a sense of whether Altruvex is the right partner, and a next step that matches the actual complexity of the work.
points: Whether an audit is needed first
A realistic proposal or estimate route
sections.title: What you get after the call

seo.title: A technical call with a clear purpose

auditOffer.description: A defined first engagement before any rebuild or scale-up. We examine the current system, isolate technical risk, and convert findings into an execution-ready roadmap.
auditOffer.title: Technical Audit -

seo.body: Technical consulting is for teams that need a clear engineering decision before they invest in a rebuild, migration, portal, ecommerce system, or performance recovery plan. Altruvex reviews the current state, identifies risk, and turns technical uncertainty into a practical roadmap.

items.a: Start with consulting when the problem is unclear, the system already exists, or multiple build paths are possible. Start with development when the scope, architecture, and success criteria are already defined.
items.q: When should we start with consulting instead of development?

items.a: A technical audit can cover architecture, performance, SEO implementation, Core Web Vitals, security surface, deployment flow, dependencies, CMS setup, integrations, and code maintainability.
items.q: What can a technical audit include?

items.a: Yes. The audit is designed to produce a roadmap that can be implemented by Altruvex, your internal team, or another developer. You keep the findings and recommendations.
items.q: Can we use the audit without hiring Altruvex for the build ?

items.a: Most focused audits take five business days after access and context are provided. Larger systems may need a longer discovery window if there are multiple codebases, integrations, or stakeholders.

sections.body: We begin by mapping what exists today: the stack, hosting, content model, integrations, performance profile, user journeys, and operational pain points. The goal is to see the system as it actually behaves, not only as it was intended to work.
points: Performance and Core Web Vitals issues
sections.title: What we inspect

sections.body: A useful audit does not stop at a list of problems. It ranks findings by business impact, technical risk, and implementation sequence so the team knows what to fix first and what can safely wait.
sections.title: What you receive

sections.body: Consulting is valuable before a major website rebuild, a Next.js migration, a custom portal, a bilingual architecture decision, an ecommerce relaunch, or any project where hidden technical debt could distort budget and timeline.
points: Before committing to a platform choice
sections.title: When it is useful

seo.title: Technical clarity before expensive decisions

body.p1: Our platform has run on a single Django application for six years. Deployment cycles are 48 minutes end-to-end.
body.p1_extra: because all squads deploy as one unit.
body.p1_hl1: Teams block each other on every release
body.p2_extra: We have 14 domain areas and just need the extraction sequence.
body.p3: All 11 teams write directly to the same
body.p3_extra: No service owns its data. We also have
body.p3_tracing: no distributed tracing across service boundaries.
body.p4_extra: but we cannot isolate which call chain is responsible. Budget is not the constraint - time is.

cta.body: Want us to run this analysis on your system?

brief.hook: We diagnose why your system is slowing you down - then fix it.

action.body: Run the extraction sequence only after tracing coverage and service ownership are in place. Start with low-dependency domains before high-coupling flows.
action.label: Action

connected.body: Shared database and missing tracing are the same root: no service owns its data, so none can be independently deployed.
connected.label: Connected

finding.body: Instrument first - you cannot safely extract a service whose failure modes you cannot trace. Then Strangler Fig: Auth → Inventory → Orders last.
finding.label: Finding → Action

question.body: How many cross-domain joins in the shared schema? That count determines extraction sequence per service.
question.label: Question

rflag.body: Premature decomposition creates a distributed monolith with identical coupling - 14 services, same problems.
rflag.label: Red flag

symptom.body: Release coupling, not microservices. Teams move at the speed of the slowest deploy - investigate topology before decomposition.
symptom.label: Symptom

brief.scrollHintDesktop: scroll to read through the engagement
brief.subtitle: Every annotation is a decision point. No recommendations before the debt is fully understood.
brief.title: The summary, read it

nextjs.description: Full-stack React framework powering every project. App Router, server components, ISR, and edge runtime in one coherent system.

nodejs.description: Server runtime for API routes, background jobs, webhooks, and serverless functions. Non-blocking I/O at the core.

postgresql.description: Production-grade relational database. ACID compliant, with JSON column support, full-text search, and row-level security.

prisma.description: Type-safe database client - schema is the single source of truth, TypeScript types generated automatically. No raw SQL drift.

react.description: Component-based UI layer. Every interface is composable, reusable, and predictably state-driven with concurrent rendering.

tailwind.description: Utility-first CSS with design tokens baked in. Fast iteration, zero dead styles in production builds, and first-class dark mode.

typescript.description: Strict type safety across the entire codebase - APIs, database queries, and UI components all share a single type system.

vercel.description: Cloud platform for serverless deployment, automated CI/CD pipelines, dynamic edge scaling, and instant global delivery.

vps.description: Virtual Private Server for fully custom infrastructure, dedicated resources, and high-performance server configurations.

1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
1.description:
By engaging Altruvex for custom web development, ERP systems, or related services, you accept these terms. These terms govern the relationship between you ('Client') and Altruvex ('we' or 'us'). If you don't agree with any term, do not proceed with a project engagement.

These terms apply to all project engagements unless a written contract supersedes them. In the case of conflict, the signed project agreement takes precedence.

2.title: 2. Service Description
2.description:
Altruvex builds custom web systems using Next.js, Turborepo, and related modern technologies. Our services include:

• Custom website design and development
• ERP and business system design
• E-commerce platforms with payment integration
• Multilingual architecture
• Integration with local and international payment systems
• Post-launch maintenance and support (optional)

We do NOT provide:

• Template-based WordPress or Shopify builds
• Ongoing hosting/SaaS (we hand off deployable code)
• 24/7 emergency support (unless specified in retainer)
• AI training data or personal data sharing with third parties

3.title: 3. Project Scope & Deliverables
3.description:
Project scope is defined in writing during discovery and confirmed in a statement of work (SOW). Scope includes:

• Specific features, integrations, and deliverables
• Number of revisions or rounds of feedback
• Timeline and key milestones
• Acceptance criteria

Changes to scope are handled as follows:

• Minor changes (within 5% effort) are included in the project
• Moderate changes (5-25% effort) require a change order and timeline adjustment
• Major changes (>25% effort) are quoted as new work or Phase 2

Any scope change must be documented and agreed in writing before work begins. Verbal requests are not binding.

4.title: 4. Payment & Pricing
4.description:
Payment follows a milestone-based structure:

• **Deposit (30-40%)**: Secures your build window and begins discovery
• **Mid-Project (30%)**: Due upon completion of design approval and architecture sign-off
• **Final (30%)**: Due upon delivery to production

Payment Terms:

• Invoices are due within 7 days unless otherwise agreed
• Late payments accrue 1.5% monthly interest
• Currency: EGP for local clients, USD for international clients
• All prices exclude third-party service fees (Stripe, Fawry, hosting, SMS, etc.), which are only billed if used
• Deposits are non-refundable if you cancel after work begins

We reserve the right to pause work on outstanding invoices.

5.title: 5. Intellectual Property & Code Ownership
5.description:
This is core to how Altruvex operates:

• **You own 100% of the source code** generated during the project
• **You own 100% of your data** in databases and storage
• **You own all custom logic and architecture** we design for your business
• We retain ownership of our methodology, frameworks, and reusable components (which are available under open-source licenses like MIT)

Deliverables include:

• Complete source code in a Git repository under your control
• Full deployment documentation
• Environment variables and secrets management
• Database schema and migration history
• Architecture decision log

You may:

• Modify, extend, or hire others to maintain the code
• Deploy to your own infrastructure or any hosting provider
• Use the code for any lawful purpose
• Fork or modify the code without permission

You may NOT:

• Claim we built it (though you can credit us)
• Use it to provide the same service to competitors without permission

6.title: 6. Warranties & Disclaimers
6.description:
What we guarantee:

• Code will follow industry best practices and modern standards
• Performance will meet targets stated in the SOW (e.g., <1s load time)
• Systems will be deployed and tested before handoff
• We'll provide 30 days of post-launch support for critical fixes
• Code will be documented and maintainable by a competent developer

What we don't guarantee:

• Traffic, conversions, or sales from the system
• Compatibility with future versions of third-party libraries (we control the build; you control the deployment)
• Protection from your own security negligence (lost credentials, weak passwords, unpatched vulnerabilities on your side)
• Compatibility with custom code you or others add after handoff
• Third-party service uptime (e.g., Stripe outages, Fawry downtime)

Post-handoff maintenance is YOUR responsibility unless you sign a maintenance retainer agreement.

7.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
7.description:
To the maximum extent allowed by law:

• Altruvex's total liability for any claim arising from this engagement is limited to the total fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim
• We are not liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, including lost profits, lost data, or lost revenue
• These limitations apply even if we've been advised of the possibility of such damages

Exceptions (unlimited liability):

• Gross negligence or willful misconduct
• Violations of intellectual property rights
• Breach of data protection obligations (GDPR, CCPA, Egyptian law)

8.title: 8. Termination & Cancellation
8.description:
Either party may terminate with written notice if:

• The other party materially breaches these terms and fails to cure within 14 days of written notice
• The other party becomes insolvent or files for bankruptcy

Cancellation by Client:

• Before work starts: Deposit is refunded, minus any costs incurred
• After work starts: Deposit is non-refundable; remaining fees are due for work completed
• If you cancel mid-project, we deliver code and documentation in its current state
• We have no obligation to complete unstarted phases

Cancellation by Altruvex:

• We may pause work for non-payment (after 14 days overdue)
• We may terminate for repeated scope creep beyond agreed change order limits
• Upon termination, we deliver all work-to-date and transition in good faith

9.title: 9. Confidentiality
9.description:
During the project, you may share:

• Business processes, workflows, and strategies
• Customer data or employee information (for system design)
• Financial or operational details

We agree to:

• Treat this information as confidential
• Not disclose it to third parties without consent
• Use it only for project delivery
• Protect it using industry-standard security (encryption, access controls)

We are NOT liable if you share:

• Passwords, API keys, or credentials (we'll never ask for these directly)
• Unencrypted sensitive data over insecure channels
• Data you don't have the right to share

After the project ends, we retain backups for 30 days for disaster recovery, then delete them. The code itself is yours forever.

10.title: 10. Dispute Resolution
10.description:
If a dispute arises:

• **First step**: Contact hello@altruvex.com with a written description of the issue
• **Negotiation period**: We'll attempt to resolve within 14 days
• **Escalation**: If unresolved, either party may pursue mediation or arbitration

For Egyptian clients:

• Disputes will be resolved under Egyptian law
• Venue is Cairo, Egypt
• We'll attempt local mediation before litigation

For international clients:

• Disputes will be resolved under the laws of the country where Altruvex is registered (Egypt)
• You agree to binding arbitration via UNCITRAL rules
• No class actions or class arbitration

Both parties agree not to pursue legal action until good-faith negotiation has been attempted.

11.title: 11. General Terms
11.description:
Entire Agreement:
These terms, combined with any signed statement of work, constitute the entire agreement. Any previous discussions or agreements are superseded.

Amendments:
We may update these terms by posting a new version. Continued engagement means you accept the new terms. If you disagree, terminate in writing.

Severability:
If any provision is invalid, the remaining terms continue to apply.

Assignment:
You may not assign your rights under this agreement without our consent. We may assign to a successor company.

Force Majeure:
We are not liable for delays or failures caused by events beyond our control (government action, war, natural disaster, pandemics). We'll notify you within 48 hours and work to minimize impact.

12.title: 12. Questions or Disputes
12.description: If you have questions about these terms or need to report a dispute, contact us at hello@altruvex.com or phone +20 (Egypt).

transparency.description: This is an initial estimate to help you plan. For a precise proposal tailored to your specific goals, Schedule a Briefing.

seo.body: Transparency is designed to make early project planning less vague. The estimator gives a realistic starting range for custom web development by looking at the kind of system you need, the maturity of your brand and content, the complexity of the workflows, and the pressure around launch timing.

sections.body: The estimate is not a fixed quote. It is a planning range that helps you understand whether the project is closer to a focused marketing site, a multilingual content system, a commerce platform, or a deeper operational product build.
sections.title: What the estimator measures

sections.body: Custom systems vary because the work is not only page count. Real effort comes from architecture, bilingual UX, payment flows, dashboards, data models, permissions, third-party integrations, QA, deployment, and handover documentation.
points: CMS, CRM, booking, or payment integrations
sections.title: Why the range can change

sections.body: Use the result to decide whether to continue with a discovery call, request a technical audit, or refine the scope before committing budget. The goal is a clearer decision before anyone writes a proposal.
points: A practical next step for the project
sections.title: How to use the result

seo.title: A clearer way to plan a custom web build

faq.title: Questions About Building Your Tech Stack
faq.subtitle: How we work, what we build, and how to get started.

01.question: Why should I trust a new company with my entire tech stack?
01.answer:
Because we do the opposite of what most agencies do. You receive your complete source code, full documentation, and zero vendor lock-in by default. If we disappeared tomorrow, your team could maintain it, or hire anyone else to. That's not a feature - it's the only honest way to build.

Technically: We use Next.js and Turborepo (both battle-tested at scale), deploy to Vercel or your own infrastructure, and structure every project so a competent developer can understand it in a week. We don't hide behind frameworks or proprietary tooling.

02.question: What happens to my code and data if Altruvex shuts down?
02.answer:
You own everything. All source code, databases, configuration, and credentials are yours from day one. We deliver a handover package that includes:

  • Complete source code (deployed exactly as it runs in production)
  • Database schema and migration history
  • Environment variables and secrets management guide
  • Deployment documentation (how to redeploy or migrate infrastructure)
  • Architecture decision log (why we chose each technology)
  • Runbook for common operations

Your code lives in a Git repository you control. We never hold it hostage.

03.question: Why custom-built instead of WordPress, Shopify, or Odoo?
03.answer:
Templates trade flexibility for speed. They're great for specific use cases (Shopify for pure e-commerce, WordPress for blogs). But if your business has custom workflows, local payment integrations, or growth that doesn't fit a plugin architecture, templates hit a ceiling you can't break through.

Custom engineering means:

  • Performance: A Next.js app loads in <1s. WordPress with plugins typically loads in 3-5s.
  • Integration: We connect directly to your bank's API, Egyptian payment processors, local suppliers - not through a plugin that approximates it.
  • Ownership: You're not locked into monthly SaaS fees or plugin vendor updates.
  • Growth: Architecture scales with your business logic, not around a plugin limitation.

04.question: What's the timeline from 'I want to start' to 'we're live'?
04.answer:
Typical timeline:

  • Week 1: Discovery (requirements, workflows, integrations, scope confirmation)
  • Week 2-3: Design review and technical architecture approval
  • Week 4-8: Development, testing, and staging deployment
  • Week 8-9: Launch, monitoring, and post-launch support

Actual timeline depends on complexity. A focused website (Essential tier) might launch in 4 weeks. A full operational system with dashboards and integrations (Flagship tier) typically takes 8-12 weeks.

We don't pad timelines. If we think something can be done in 6 weeks, we'll tell you 6 weeks - not 12.

05.question: What if my needs change after we've started?
05.answer:
Scope changes are built into our process. We structure projects in phases, so:

  • Essential features ship first, then integrations, then dashboards.
  • If you realize you need something different mid-project, we rebuild that phase before moving forward - not after.
  • We estimate the cost impact upfront so there are no surprises.

After launch, the 30-day warranty covers critical fixes. Structural changes move to a maintenance retainer or a new project quote, depending on complexity.

06.question: How do you handle Egyptian and Arab market integrations?
06.answer:
This is core to what we do. We've built integrations with:

  • Fawry (bill payment and e-commerce)
  • Vodafone Cash (mobile payments)
  • Bank transfers (local and SWIFT)
  • Local delivery and logistics APIs
  • Arabic-language content and RTL workflows

We understand the peculiarities: payment processors that need local IDs, SMS systems that charge per message, hosting that needs local data residency. These aren't afterthoughts - they're part of the architecture from discovery.

07.question: Is the website actually multilingual (all supported languages), or just mirrored?
07.answer:
True multilingual, not mirrored.

Most sites build in English, then translate. We design and engineer for both languages simultaneously:

  • RTL (right-to-left) is native, not a CSS flip.
  • Arabic content isn't a translated afterthought - it's an equal-priority experience.
  • Forms, dashboards, and workflows handle both languages without breaking layouts.
  • Performance is identical in both languages (no slower Arabic load times).

This matters if your customers or employees are primarily Arabic-speaking. A bad Arabic UX loses credibility.

08.question: What happens after the 30-day post-launch warranty ends?
08.answer:
You have three options:

  1. Maintenance Retainer (Recommended for most): Essential tier is 2,000 EGP/month, Professional is 4,000 EGP/month. Includes monitoring, security updates, performance optimization, and bug fixes. Typical turnaround for non-critical issues: 2-5 business days.
  2. As-Needed Support: No monthly commitment. We bill hourly for changes and fixes (charged at project rates). Typical for businesses that stabilize quickly.
  3. Bring Your Own Team: We hand off complete documentation so you or a developer you hire can maintain it independently. We're available for consultations if needed.

Most clients choose the retainer because it means someone's always watching for performance degradation or security issues.

09.question: Can we hire a different developer to maintain our system later?
09.answer:
Yes. That's why we build the way we do.

The handover package makes it possible for any competent full-stack developer to take over. They'll understand:

  • The technology choices (Next.js, databases, deployment strategy)
  • The business logic (why workflows are structured the way they are)
  • The operational concerns (monitoring, error tracking, scaling)

We typically do a 4-6 hour knowledge transfer call, then your developer is on their own. Some clients do this immediately after launch; others wait 2 years. It's your call.

10.question: What if I need something complex that doesn't fit the pricing tiers?
10.answer:
Custom quotes exist for a reason. The tiers (Essential, Professional, Flagship, E-commerce) are reference points, not ceilings.

Common custom scenarios:

  • Multi-tenant SaaS platform (different from single-business dashboard)
  • Complex ERP integration (SAP, NetSuite, Odoo connectivity)
  • Real-time analytics or reporting engine
  • API-first architecture for third-party integrations
  • Hybrid mobile + web system

We scope these in discovery, then quote based on engineering complexity, timeline, and infrastructure needs. If it's genuinely custom, you get a custom price.

11.question: How do you charge for projects? What's the payment schedule?
11.answer:
Milestone-based, not time-and-materials:

  • Deposit (typically 30-40% of total): Secures your build window
  • Mid-project (typically 30%): Upon completion of discovery and design approval
  • Final (typically 30%): Upon delivery to production

No hidden fees. Your quote includes:

  • Domain and SSL setup
  • Base hosting (Vercel or your infrastructure)
  • Deployment and CI/CD
  • Post-launch monitoring for 30 days

Third-party tools (Stripe, Fawry, SMS services, analytics) are only billed if your project actually uses them - at their standard rates, not marked up.

12.question: Can we start small and expand later?
12.answer:
Yes. This is the entire point of modular architecture.

Example: Start with Essential tier (simple website), then 6 months later add Professional features (CMS, integrations, dashboards). The codebase is designed so expansions don't require a rebuild.

We track this in a technical roadmap that's part of your handover. Phase 2 work is typically quoted before Phase 1 ends, so you know what the full vision costs and timelines.

13.question: How do you measure success? What are the guarantees?
13.answer:
We measure against what actually matters to your business:

  • Performance: <1s load time on mobile (Core Web Vitals targets)
  • Uptime: 99.5%+ availability (monitored and reported monthly)
  • Conversion: Forms work, integrations process correctly, customers can complete actions
  • Maintainability: A developer unfamiliar with your codebase can fix bugs in a day

We don't guarantee traffic or sales - that's marketing, not engineering. We guarantee the system works, performs, and is maintainable.

Post-launch, these metrics are in your monthly maintenance report. If performance degrades, we fix it under the maintenance retainer.

14.question: What's your actual experience? Can you show me projects?
14.answer:
We've built:

  • Altruvex.com - Our own website (Web Engineering, 2025). Built with Next.js, Turborepo, deployed to Vercel.
  • NewLight Lighting Store - E-commerce with Fawry integration, inventory management, multi-tier workflows (2024)
  • Art Lighting Store - E-commerce platform with advanced filtering and performance optimization (2024)

You can examine these live to understand our engineering standards. We don't use stock imagery or templates - what you see is what we build.

For more detailed case studies and technical breakdowns, check the Work section on our site.
